D6 Display L4 Analyze Reference

How Browsers Tag Colour

a browser only converts an asset if it carries a colour profile — untagged, it assumes sRGB, and a wide-gamut image drifts off its intended colour

When a browser paints an image or CSS colour, it asks: is there a colour profile? If yes, it converts the numbers from that profile to the display. If no, it assumes sRGB. An untagged wide-gamut photo is therefore shown with its raw numbers read as sRGB — and the wider the asset's true gamut, the further the colour shifts from what was intended.

Toggle the colour tag and widen the asset's gamut. Tagged, the swatch holds its intended colour; untagged, it drifts as the source saturation grows. CSS color() and tagged images are handled; bare numbers default to sRGB.
